name: CONVERT
description: Data type converter - converts signal between different numeric types

exprs.description: exprs[0]=input type, exprs[1]=output type, exprs[2]=do rounding

explicitOutputs.description: Output with converted type and auto-dimension

xmlExample:
<BasicBlock id="b1" parent="0:2:0" interfaceFunctionName="CONVERT" blockType="c" dependsOnU="1" dependsOnT="0" simulationFunctionName="convert" simulationFunctionType="C_OR_FORTRAN" style="CONVERT">
  <ScilabString as="exprs" height="3" width="1">
    <data line="0" column="0" value="1"/>
    <data line="1" column="0" value="3"/>
    <data line="2" column="0" value="0"/>
  </ScilabString>
  <ScilabInteger as="integerParameters" height="1" width="1" intPrecision="sci_int32">
    <data line="0" column="0" value="2"/>
  </ScilabInteger>
  <mxGeometry as="geometry" x="0.0" y="-10.0" width="40.0" height="40.0"/>
</BasicBlock>
anomalies: No realParameters - uses integerParameters for type code
Special negative values for dimension handling
criticalRules: exprs[2]=0: truncate, 1: round when converting to integer
dataColumns=-2 enables auto-dimensioning for vector/matrix inputs
Converts both data type and can round/truncate values
commonUses: Converting between double and integer types
